div.style: display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
color: rgb(94, 61, 18) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Each worker gets their OWN private notebook.
Nobody can read anyone else's.
=> saved trips: a smaller number

div.style: color: rgb(28, 82, 48) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
All workers share ONE whiteboard.
Worker 2 can read what Worker 1 wrote.
=> saved trips: a BIG number

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
THE HONEST TEST — how we PROVE the whiteboard helped
(we run the exact same work TWICE)

div.style: color: rgb(51, 66, 79) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Take the same team's work
(the same questions, same order)

div.style: color: rgb(27, 58, 102) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
THE DIFFERENCE (A minus B)
= exactly what SHARING bought.
This is 'cross-agent uplift'.

div.style: color: rgb(107, 84, 16) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Why this is honest:
the kernel COUNTS the saved trips itself.
We don't model or guess them.
It is a real before/after swap.

div.style: color: rgb(28, 82, 48) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Sanity check: if the workers share
NOTHING (all different questions),
the difference must be ZERO.
We measured exactly 0 in all 2,500 cells. ✓
